title: Indulgent Brazilian Cashew Pudding
description: A silky, whole30-friendly pudding made from cashews, coconut milk, and frozen mango, finished with a touch of celery salt and lime zest for a cultured, molten experience.
instructions: Drain the soaked cashews and place them in a high‑speed blender with water and coconut oil.
Blend until the cashews form a smooth, creamy base.
Add the coconut milk and frozen mango chunks to the blender and pulse until fully incorporated.
Stir in the celery salt, vanilla extract, and ground cinnamon until evenly mixed.
Pour the mixture into a heat‑proof bowl and place it over a pot of simmering water to create a double boiler.
Heat the mixture gently, whisking constantly, until it thickens to a ganache‑like consistency, about 15 minutes.
Remove the bowl from the heat and stir in lime zest to brighten the flavor.
Transfer the pudding into individual serving cups or a shallow dish and smooth the tops.
Let the pudding rest at room temperature for 15 minutes to set and develop a molten center.
Garnish each serving with a fresh mint leaf before serving.
